Document:
# Path of Flesh
Difficulty: [color=yellow]Varies[/color]
The Path of Flesh revolves around summoning ghouls and monstrosities to do your bidding. Pick this path if you enjoy the fantasy of being a necromancer commanding legions of allies.
## Pros
- Can turn dead humanoids into fragile but loyal ghouls. - Strong mark effect. - Access to a versatile list of summoned minions. - Your summons are very versatile and can quickly overwhelm the crew should you coordinate your attacks - Eating organs and body parts grants various boons (depending on your path stage).
## Cons
- A high degree of your progression is obtaining additional summoned monsters. - You have very little utility beyond your summoned monsters. - You gain no inherent access to defensive, offensive or mobility spells. - You are mostly focused around supporting your minions.
## Knowledge
Knowledge Name Ingredients/Type Description
Principle of Hunger Knife, Blood Creates a bloody blade.
Grasp of Flesh Passive Ability Your grasp can raise the dead as loyal ghouls.
Mark of Flesh Passive Ability Your grasp will now apply the flesh mark, creating flesh mimic of the victim that attack them when triggered.
Imperfect Ritual Corpse, Muzzle Raises the corpse as a Voiceless Dead, a strong, but silent minion.
Knitting of Flesh Active Ability Grants a spell that works as all surgical tools, heals minions, and provides health analysis and hud.
Raw Ritual Eyes, Stomach, Blood Summons a raw prophet, an entity that can link your summons minds via the mansus link. It is also a strong scout and heals heretics and monsters around it.
Bleeding Steel Passive Ability Your bloody blades now cause heavy bleeding on attack.
Lonely Ritual Heart, Stomach, Lung, Pen Summons a flesh stalker, a strong combat summon with a jaunt, shapeshift and EMP.
Priest's Final Hymn Four corpses You can now shapeshift into extremely powerful creature. Ghoul limit increased. Knit Flesh can now be used in hand to heal ghouls in area.
## Armor and Passive Ability
Name Effect
T1 Passive You heal from eating raw meat and organs, as well as become immune to diseases and damage from poisons.
Writhing Embrace Chance to split into loyal flesh mimic upon taking damage.
T2 Passive Ghoul limit increased.
T3 Passive Medical hud.
## Tips
- Your Mansus Grasp allows you to turn dead humanoids into ghouls (even mindshielded humanoids like security officers and the captain). It also Leaves a mark that spawns a loyal flesh mimic when triggered by your bloody blade. - As a Flesh Heretic, organs and dead bodies are your best friends! You can use them for rituals, to heal or to gain buffs. - You can recall your ghouls or summonned creatures by [color=yellow]Alt-Clicking[/color] the ritual rune using your mansus grasp. - Your Flesh Surgery spell can heal your summons. It also acts like a good surgery tool that bypasses surgery steps and works through clothes. - Raw Prophets can link you and other summons in a telepathic network, allowing for long distance co-ordination. They also have healing aura for you and your minions. - Flesh Stalkers are decent combatants with the ability to disguise themselves as various creatures. They can also utilize an EMP spell. - Your success with this path is reliant on how knowledgable or robust your minions are. However, there is always power in numbers; the more minions, the higher your chances of success. - Your minions are more expendable than you are. Do not be afraid to tell them to go to their deaths. You can just recover them later... maybe.
